title: NestJS circular dependency injection
desc: Use forwardRef when two services depend on each other directly. Otherwise restructure with a shared module to break the cycle.
descAr: استخدم forwardRef عندما يعتمد كل من خدمتين على الأخرى مباشرة. أو أعد الهيكلة باستخدام وحدة مشتركة لكسر الدورة.
summary: Two services injecting each other causes NestJS to throw at startup. forwardRef lazy-resolves the token after the module initialises — or better, eliminate the cycle with a shared module.
problem: Two services injecting each other directly causes NestJS to throw 'Cannot resolve dependency' at startup because the DI container cannot construct a circular object graph. This is common with AuthService and UserService where each needs the other's methods. The error message points to the constructor parameters, so it's easy to identify which dependency causes the cycle. But simply swapping imports doesn't fix the architectural issue.
solution: Apply @Inject(forwardRef(() => ServiceName)) on one side of the circular dependency. NestJS lazy-resolves the forwardRef token after the module is fully initialised. Better yet, extract the shared logic into a third module to eliminate the cycle entirely. This is cleaner and avoids the indirection of forwardRef.
solutionTitle: Use forwardRef or restructure into a shared module

callout.text: Key idea: forwardRef is a band-aid, not a design pattern. If you use it, add a comment explaining why the cycle exists so the next developer knows it's intentional.

code.html: @Injectable() export class AuthService { constructor( @Inject(forwardRef(() => UsersService)) private users: UsersService, ) {} }

codeSteps.label: Register both providers in the module
codeSteps.html: @Module({ providers: [AuthService, UsersService], exports: [AuthService, UsersService], }) export class AuthModule {}

baDesc: The structural change is minimal, but the DI container behaves entirely differently.

before.desc: NestJS throws 'Cannot resolve dependency' at startup.
before.code: // Circular — both inject each other directly @Injectable() export class AuthService { constructor( private users: UsersService, // throws! ) {} }

after.desc: Both services initialise and can call each other's methods.
after.code: // One side uses forwardRef @Injectable() export class AuthService { constructor( @Inject(forwardRef(() => UsersService)) private users: UsersService, ) {} }
